a_image.alt: Solarpunk style mood board — Subculture · Green Future

sections.a_description: Lush greens, warm sunlight yellows, and the infectious optimism of a future where solar panels grow alongside vines — Solarpunk design imagines technology that coexists with nature rather than replacing it. The organic rounded surfaces, verdant accents, and warm cream backgrounds create digital spaces that feel like community gardens powered by renewable energy.

This hopeful style powers sustainability-focused startups, renewable energy companies, urban farming platforms, and eco-community networks. You'll see it on climate action organizations, green technology showcases, sustainable fashion brands, and community cooperative sites — anywhere that the visual language needs to communicate ecological optimism and the belief that a greener future is not just possible but beautiful.

The optimistic, nature-tech fusion aesthetic of solarpunk — lush greens, warm sunlight yellows, and the hopeful vision of a future where technology and nature coexist harmoniously. Background: warm cream (#faf7f0) like sun-warmed paper. The .inner panel has a soft green-tinted background (#f0f7ec) with a 1px border (#c5deb8) and 16px border-radius — organic and rounded, like leaves and solar cells. Colors: vibrant green (#3d8b37) as the primary accent — the green of healthy plants, sustainable energy, and ecological optimism. Warm dark (#2d3a29) for headings, forest gray (#5a6e55) for body text. A secondary gold (#d4a017) for warmth accents. The aesthetic is hopeful and organic — technology that looks like it grew rather than was manufactured. Set in Nunito — rounded and friendly, with the approachable warmth that matches solarpunk's optimistic worldview. Heading at weight 700, 26px, line-height 1.3, color #2d3a29. Eyebrow at weight 600, 11px, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color #3d8b37. Body at weight 400, 15px, line-height 1.7, color #5a6e55. Buttons: primary — green (#3d8b37), white text, 12px border-radius. Secondary: transparent, green border. Alternatives: Comfortaa (rounded optimistic), Lexend (accessible friendly).
{What this palette represents — the core identity}
